competitive_position.summary: Zycus is competing in a four-finalist rapid evaluation (Zycus, Zip, Renpayments, Payhawk/Airwallix) for a P2P + payment automation deal where payment capability is the buyer's #1 pain and Zycus lacks a native payment module. Primary threat is Renpayments (CFO-sponsored POC concluding imminently, outcome unknown). Secondary threat is Zip (procurement-focused, similar to Zycus, price-competitive). Zycus strengths: end-to-end P2P, AI (Merlin/ANA), operational advocate (David + team favor Zycus per Next Step 'Rest of the team really like Zycus'). Zycus vulnerabilities: no native payment module (buyer stated disappointment 3 June), payment platform in development with no launch date or reference customers, mitigation is partner integration (adds cost, may push total above £250k budget threshold), and no CFO engagement despite CFO running Ren POC. Competitive wedge: position Zycus as end-to-end P2P + AI + payment partner integration vs payment-only platforms (Ren, Payhawk, Airwallix) and vs Zip on AI depth. How we win: price competitively (Zycus + partner integration under £250k and under Ren all-in cost), de-position Zip on AI roadmap and offer to build missing features free, position Ren as payment-only (if true) vs Zycus breadth, and secure CFO overlay (Anthony Gray) to counter Ren POC momentum before decision early July. No competitive review conducted in 30+ days despite active POC and finalist advancement.

champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole (David Hogsden, Evaluator); email david.hogsden-ext@yondrgroup.com (interim per -ext suffix); Next Step ~15 June ('Rest of the team really like Zycus'); LastActivityDate 30 June; prior record (David reports to VP Finance, no CFO access evidence)
champion_strength.summary: Champion is David Hogsden, Interim AP Senior Manager, operational advocate with active engagement (last contact 30 June, 1 day ago). David initiated evaluation, provided use-case requirements, attended discovery and demo calls, and stated 'Rest of the team really like Zycus' (Next Step ~15 June), signaling internal support. However, David is interim contractor (per -ext email suffix) and may rotate out before February 2027 go-live (8 months from now), risking advocacy continuity. David lacks executive access: no evidence of introducing Zycus to CFO or VP Finance, and CFO is running parallel Renpayments POC without David's apparent influence to block or position against it. Champion strength is developing: David is a coach and operational advocate but lacks executive-level access and political capital to override CFO preference if Ren POC succeeds. At risk due to interim status and lack of executive overlay. Recommended action: secure executive sponsorship (Anthony Gray to CFO) to supplement David's operational advocacy with executive-level influence and de-risk his interim status.
champion_strength.champion: David Hogsden, Interim Accounts Payable Senior Manager

sources: Next Step ~15 June 2026 (CFO running Renpayments POC)
24 June 2026 call ('budget is zero… get it as cheap as you can' per prior record)
Prior record identifying CFO first name Sandy
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is the CFO (first name Sandy per prior record, last name unknown). The CFO holds final budget authority and is actively evaluating competing solutions: as of mid-June the CFO initiated a 2-week proof-of-concept with Renpayments (per Next Step dated ~15 June: 'The CFO is looking into Renpayments - currently running a 2 week POC'). This POC is concluding imminently (late June / early July) and its outcome will heavily influence the final decision. The CFO has not been engaged directly by Zycus or attended any Zycus calls/demos in the available record, which is a red flag given the CFO is running a parallel competitive evaluation. David Hogsden (operational champion) stated 'Rest of the team really like Zycus, they have not had the deep dive with Ren yet' (Next Step ~15 June), signaling that David and his peers favor Zycus but the CFO may favor Ren if the POC succeeds. Budget threshold is ~£250k for RFP (per Next Step); buyer also said 'budget is zero… get it as cheap as you can' (24 June per prior record), indicating extreme cost sensitivity. The CFO's passive sponsorship (running competitor POC, no direct Zycus engagement) is a vulnerability. Zycus must secure executive overlay (Anthony Gray to CFO) to position against Ren POC outcome and demonstrate cost-effectiveness before the CFO makes final decision.

recommended_moves: Deliver commercial pricing pack with payment partner integration cost (Transfermate or alternative) to David Hogsden by end of day 2 July, ensuring total cost (Zycus license + partner integration) is under £250k budget threshold and competitive vs Renpayments all-in pricing. Include implementation timeline confirmation (6-month deployment achievable with payment partner or phased approach: P2P first, payments later).

Secure executive overlay: Anthony Gray (deal owner's manager) to schedule CFO call by 4 July to counter Renpayments POC momentum, position Zycus end-to-end P2P + AI + payment partner integration vs Ren payment-only platform (if true), and demonstrate executive-level commitment to buyer success. Use call to understand Ren POC outcome, CFO's decision criteria, and budget sensitivity, and propose Zycus differentiation (Merlin/I2O AI roadmap, feature build offer, P2P breadth).

De-position Zip on AI depth in upcoming workshop: demonstrate Merlin negotiation agent (AI-driven supplier price benchmarking and negotiation playbook), I2O agentic purchasing roadmap (autonomous PO creation, intelligent routing), and ANA approval intelligence (context-aware approval workflows, exception prediction) vs Zip's AI capability. Offer to build any missing features Zip has that Zycus lacks, free of cost pre-contract, to neutralize feature parity and win on AI differentiation and buyer-specific commitment. Workshop scheduled as next step per Next Step 'Demo went well - p2p deal - next steps is a workshop'.
